input_text:
We tried switching internal service communication to gRPC. Spent a week on the migration — updated the service definitions, generated client/server stubs, converted the main inter-service calls.

Then hit a wall: our infrastructure runs Nginx as a reverse proxy, and the standard Nginx build doesn't support gRPC (HTTP/2 framing). Getting gRPC through Nginx requires either a custom Nginx build or upgrading to Nginx 1.13.10+ with the grpc_pass module. Our managed Kubernetes provider doesn't easily support custom Nginx builds, and the upgrade path involves a maintenance window.

Reverting everything back to REST/JSON. Keeping the gRPC investigation findings in a decision doc for if/when we revisit.

expected.text: Revert internal service communication from gRPC back to REST/JSON — Nginx reverse proxy doesn't support gRPC framing without custom build/upgrade, which our managed K8s provider can't easily accommodate. Keep gRPC findings for a future revisit

reviewer_notes: Stack-choice decision to reverse a previous direction due to infrastructure constraints. The institutional knowledge ('we tried gRPC and it failed because of X') is exactly the kind of thing future devs need.
